is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of uncomplicated falciparum malaria; children with microscopically confirmed monoinfection of Plasmodium falciparum.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports point mutations on dhfr codon 108 and dhps codon 437, both in the target list (Pfdhfr:108, Pfdhps:437).

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 3: dhfr(108) Resistant 104/113 (92.0%), Mixed 6, Sensitive 3; dhps(437) Resistant 50/113 (44.3%), Mixed 17, Sensitive 46.

sub_country_location.evidence: All samples from clinic H, Maheba Refugee Settlement, North-Western Province, Zambia — a specific site well below country level.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Study conducted between March and June 2002; day 0 samples collected within a single year, <=3y.

african_field_samples.evidence: Human clinical isolates from children in Maheba Refugee Settlement, Zambia (African field site).

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary randomized efficacy/effectiveness trial with genomic analysis of collected samples.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Related prior adherence study on the same site (Depoortere et al. 2004), but molecular marker data appear to be a new field collection specific to this trial.

supplement.evidence: Per-locus frequency counts are fully provided in Table 3 within the main paper.

assessment.summary: Randomized efficacy/effectiveness trial of SP+artesunate in children in Maheba Refugee Settlement, Zambia (March–June 2002). All 113 day-0 PCR samples analysed for dhfr(108) and dhps(437) mutations, with extractable counts in Table 3, tied to the clinic H site and a single collection year.
